title: LRU Cache
problem_statement: Design and implement a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache with get and put operations in O(1) time.

examples.input: LRUCache cache = new LRUCache(2); cache.put(1, 1); cache.put(2, 2); cache.get(1); cache.put(3, 3); cache.get(2); cache.get(3);
examples.explanation: When capacity is exceeded, the least recently used entry is evicted.

constraints: At most 10^5 calls to get and put
intuition: Use a data structure that keeps access order updated on every get and put operation, like LinkedHashMap in access-order mode.

approaches.name: LinkedHashMap Access Order
approaches.description: Use a LinkedHashMap configured for access order so the least recently used entry moves to the front and can be evicted automatically when capacity is exceeded.
approaches.why_not: A naive list-based LRU is simpler conceptually but does not guarantee constant-time update for both get and put.

interview_qa.a: LinkedHashMap in access-order mode automatically moves accessed entries to the tail, so the least recently used entry is easy to evict.

interview_qa.q: How can get and put both be O(1)?
interview_qa.a: The underlying hash map provides fast lookup while the linked structure maintains order with constant-time updates.
